dialog.stepLabel: {PlayerName}: "(It touched the talisman then ran away. Is it trying to lead..."

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (It touched the talisman then ran away. Is it trying to lead me somewhere?)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (That direction… it's the Synchrolink Training Grounds.)

items.speaker: Curious Student
items.text: What's wrong? The simulation pod won't open?

items.speaker: Pensive Student
items.text: Seems so. It bugged out with an emergency error and just... locked down. Maintenance is on the way.

items.speaker: Surprised Student
items.text: But... it's showing it's occupied? Class just started. No one could have gotten in yet.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: ...Aemeath?

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (Aemeath said someone lifted her from the lake when she was a child. That's when she lost her talisman.)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (It wasn't my past self who saved her… it was me. Right here, right now.)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (My failure to save Aemeath… has it already become part of Lahai-Roi's chain of cause and effect?)

dialog.stepLabel: Aemeath: "Now that your frequency is stable, what will you do next? St..."

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Now that your frequency is stable, what will you do next? Stay in Lahai-Roi, maybe?

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: The Threnodian here needs to be dealt with first.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Hah, of course. Typical workaholic. I knew you'd say that.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Aleph-1… won't be an easy fight. But I believe in you. You're the one who keeps making the impossible possible.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: And after that, will you keep traveling? Any destination in mind?

options.text: I need to think about that.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Of course. Take your time.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: This world you've chosen is vast. You can go anywhere.

items.speaker: Young Aemeath
items.text: Are you leaving now?

items.speaker: Past {PlayerName}
items.text: Yes. There's something I have to do.

items.speaker: Past {PlayerName}
items.text: The Academy will look after you. Or you can go back to live in Bjartr Woods.

items.speaker: Young Aemeath
items.text: When... when will you come back?

items.speaker: Past {PlayerName}
items.text: ...I don't know, Aemeath. I'm sorry.

items.speaker: Past {PlayerName}
items.text: But I'll come back to see you when I can. I promise.

items.speaker: Young Aemeath
items.text: You promise?

items.speaker: Past {PlayerName}
items.text: Promise.

items.speaker: Young Aemeath
items.text: Then… I'll wait for you here in Lahai-Roi.

items.speaker: Past {PlayerName}
items.text: Don't feel you have to. The world is vast. Don't let me or Lahai-Roi hold you back.

items.speaker: Young Aemeath
items.text: B-but… you can live for so, so many years. What if I'm gone by the time you return—

items.speaker: Young Aemeath
items.text: …Sorry. I shouldn't have said that. You should go now.

items.speaker: Past {PlayerName}
items.text: Aemeath—

items.speaker: Young Aemeath
items.text: Just go!

items.speaker: Past {PlayerName}
items.text: I know you're worried.

items.speaker: Young Aemeath
items.text: …It's not fair. Why does it have to be you saving the world? You're always so exhausted… always getting hurt…

items.speaker: Past {PlayerName}
items.text: Look, Aemeath...

items.speaker: Past {PlayerName}
items.text: I've walked through lands torn apart by the Lament. Every tear weighs me down, and every cry breaks a bit of my heart.

items.speaker: Past {PlayerName}
items.text: Their pain is no different from mine. Neither is their joy.

items.speaker: Past {PlayerName}
items.text: So if I can help, I will. I have to.

items.speaker: Young Aemeath
items.text: Then… I'll be the one to protect you someday.

items.speaker: Past {PlayerName}
items.text: You... protect me?

items.speaker: Young Aemeath
items.text: Yes! You're not invincible! You get hurt, you get tired, you get sad… you're just like everyone else in this world.

items.speaker: Young Aemeath
items.text: So I'll protect you.

items.speaker: Young Aemeath
items.text: I'll chase off anyone who tries to hurt you! Like Katya from Space Fantasy!

items.speaker: Young Aemeath
items.text: I'll become a savior, just like you.

items.speaker: Past {PlayerName}
items.text: Hehe...

items.speaker: Young Aemeath
items.text: Don't laugh! I'm serious!

items.speaker: Past {PlayerName}
items.text: Alright, alright, I'll stop now. I know you're serious. And I'm listening to every word.

items.speaker: Past {PlayerName}
items.text: I'll be counting on you then, Aemeath.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Tired?

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: The frequency Sigillum took is coming back. It's… not a great feeling. Abby's already helping me recover through the Tacet Mark. I'll be fine soon.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: Just close your eyes and take a nap. You deserve a little peace.

items.speaker: Aemeath
items.text: You can count on me...

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Aemeath!

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (She's gone… This is the piece I was missing. Luuk said the Core's self-diagnostics would trigger peak Void Storm season.)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (She knew. When she returned the Core, she already knew. She's been waiting for this moment… waiting to travel back in time.)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (The lake in the frostlands… the Voidmatter is densest at its depths. If I'm lucky, I can find a way into Voidspace from there.)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (I can still catch up to her. I have to move, now!)

dialog.stepLabel: {PlayerName}: "(There's still a way. I can't give up here.)..."

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (There's still a way. I can't give up here.)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: This is... her paper plane.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: You can keep it, if you'd like.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: I know you're not Aemeath. You're only a piece of what she left behind, kept alive by this talisman.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: But if you exist... it means she's still alive, too. Doesn't it?

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Otherwise, the Soliskin couldn't have brought you back.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: So… it wasn't for nothing. If I hadn't gone looking for Aemeath, I never would have found this talisman.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: And you wouldn't be here at all.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Luuk told me you're a piece of her. A thread that might still tie her to this world.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: If I follow it… maybe I can find where she is.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: He might've only said that to comfort me. No one but her has ever seen what's beyond the Stridergate. It's all just guesswork.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: How long has it been for her since she was pulled into that chaos...?

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Is she still alive? And if she is… how much longer can she hold on?

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: …I don't know.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: But I will hold on to hope.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Because the world she saved is so full of it. So peaceful, and so beautiful.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: It's strange… seeing "Aemeath" so quiet like this.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: I will cross the Stridergate and bring you home.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Wait for me, Aemeath.

items.speaker: "Aemeath"
items.text: ...Don't... be sad...
A gentle breeze whispers the beginnings of spring.
